issue_title: Possible security problem with basic_auth_required() decorator.
issue_body:
It's come to my attention that for some LDAP servers, if you try to bind your connection to a user account with a valid username and an empty password, you'll get an anonymous connection - the bind doesn't fail, you just wind up with less access than you might have expected.

Therefore, if you access a paged protected by `basic_auth_required()` and provide a valid username and an empty password, the wrapper will try to bind, succeed (getting an anonymous bind) and assume that the client provided the correct username for that account.

The fix would be something like changing `req_password is None` to `req_password in [None, ""]`.

issue_title: ng-file-select: no input element on custom element
issue_body:
I'm using the wonderful [Angular Material project](https://github.com/angular/material/) for the styling of a webpage. It lets you use directives and services to apply the [Material Design principles](https://www.google.com/design/spec/material-design/) on a web page. One of these directives is the `md-button`, which can be used to create a button element that is styled conform these principles.

I have tried to use `md-button` in combination with the `ng-file-select` attribute. However, it seems that these two don't mix. When I use a regular `button` element, everything seems to work fine. See the following [plnkr](http://plnkr.co/edit/MzNYVbggjCLVM2xxZ5SS?p=preview).

As you can see in the demo the `button` element has an `input` child and the `md-button` has not. In the [angular-file-upload code](https://github.com/danialfarid/angular-file-upload/blob/5c5d7189b0722f0f4e5096cabba680b93205fc82/dist/angular-file-upload.js#L196) I can see the `input` element being added. Unfortunately, in the end it is not there as child of the `md-button`. 

I think the `md-button` directive might have replaced or removed the `input` element, but I am not sure. Maybe it has something to do with the order in which both directives are executing?

As a solution I tried to change the `.append(fileElem)` in `.after(fileElem)` and that works. Appending the `fileElem` to the document body also works, but then one would need to destroy the corresponding `input` when removing the element.

issue_title: Storage patching fails for fields on proxy models
issue_body:
It turns out that proxy models re-use field instances, which means that during the first call to `setup_storage` the wrapping gets messed up.

Suppose you have a field `image` that appears on a model that has a proxy of it. During setup_storage, the following might happen:

1. The field on the proxy model gets overridden. Default storage is stored in `previous_storages` for the field, and then LocMemStorage is set as the field's storage.
2. The field on the main model gets overridden. It's the same instance. LocMemStorage is stored in `previous_storages` for the field, and then LocMemStorage is set as the field's storage.

I think the solution here might be to ignore proxy models when overriding file fields, though it might also work to make filefields a set rather than a list.

The only workaround I've thougt of so far is in the docstring yaml change the type to a string so users could put in the literal string values of True and False, but I'd much rather have accurate documentation on what the type is

issue_title: Implement pagination
issue_body:
I had a minor disaster related to using python-digitalocean to manipulate domain records since python-digitalocean does not seem to implement pagination.

According to https://developers.digitalocean.com/documentation/v2/#links and https://developers.digitalocean.com/documentation/changelog/api-v2/tweak-per-page/ things are by default paginated 20 items per page.  Based on experimentation, this is the case with DNS records.

As a result, when it was working with a name that was not on the first "page" within its domain, my script kept failing to find an existing DNS record to modify, and created a new one instead. (yes, my script should have done other things like keep track of the numeric identifier of its record, but there you go)

When I found this out, the cloud control panel showed that I had over 12,000 records in my domain(!).  I'm cleaning things out with `doctl`, which is able to issue requests like `GET /v2/domains/…/records?page=2&per_page=200` to retrieve all the data.

issue_title: pytest flaky interaction causing fixture exceptions to be ignored
issue_body:
Somewhat related to [this issue](https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/1480) which was supposedly resolved in Flaky 3.1.1 

In the example below, something in flaky is causing the exception that is thrown inside the fixture not to be reported anymore. 
```
import pytest
import flaky

@pytest.fixture
def failing_fixture():
    raise Exception("Woops")

@flaky.flaky()
@pytest.mark.usefixtures('failing_fixture')
class TestCases:
    def test_fixture_fail(self):
        pass

    def test_always_pass(self):
        assert True

    def test_always_fail(self):
        assert False
```
The test results show that 3 tests were gathered but none were executed (which is true) but leaves no trace to the exception in the fixture. 
```

platform linux -- Python 3.5.1, pytest-3.0.6, py-1.4.32, pluggy-0.4.0
rootdir: /home/thou/mine/python_scribbles, inifile: 
plugins: flaky-3.3.0
collected 3 items 

my_pytest.py 
===Flaky Test Report===


===End Flaky Test Report===

 no tests ran in 0.02 seconds
```

If we remove the flaky decorator from the test class, the error is reported normally.

issue_title: Fix tests for new dbcopy location
issue_body:
Cloudant versions based on CouchDB 2.0 store view dbcopy information in the design document epi options config:
```javascript
{"options": {"epi": {"dbcopy": {"myview": "mycopy"}}}}
```
whereas historically it was stored in the view object:
```javascript
 {"views": {"myview": {"map": "...", "reduce": "...", "dbcopy": "mycopy"}}}
```

This means that it cannot always be retrieved from the same dict location as previously. See additional discussion:

https://github.com/cloudant/python-cloudant/pull/206#discussion_r74218882
https://github.com/cloudant/python-cloudant/pull/206#discussion_r74221218
https://github.com/cloudant/python-cloudant/pull/206#discussion_r75280808
https://github.com/cloudant/python-cloudant/pull/206#discussion_r75288949

- Cloudant (python-cloudant) version(s) that are affected by this issue.
    - 2.x
- Python version 
    - Any
- A small code sample that demonstrates the issue.
    - Trying to retrieve the value of `dbcopy` from the old location fails e.g. when running `tests.unit.design_document_tests.DesignDocumentTests.test_fetch_map_reduce` against a new version

issue_title: Sorting Competition Leaderboard by rank ( for a given set of users )
issue_body:
For ranked_in_list API in CompetitionLeaderboard if any user id doesn't exist in a leaderboard and 'sort_by' = 'rank' is used, then the API throws an exception ( 'rank' key not found ). Whereas it works if the 'sort_by' = 'score' is used.

The bug is 'rank' is not initialised to None - unlike 'score' which by default is initialised to None.
This can be fixed with the following:

data[self.RANK_KEY] = None in line 100 or so as shown here:

            score = responses[index * 2 + 1]
            if score is not None:
                score = float(score)
            data[self.SCORE_KEY] = score

            data[self.RANK_KEY] = None # This is the additional line

            if self.order == self.ASC:
                data[self.RANK_KEY] = self.redis_connection.zcount(
                    leaderboard_name, '-inf', "(%s" % str(float(data[self.SCORE_KEY])))
            else:
                #print "SCORE_KEY - " + str(data[self.SCORE_KEY])
                if data[self.SCORE_KEY] != None:
                        data[self.RANK_KEY] = self.redis_connection.zcount(
                            leaderboard_name, "(%s" % str(float(data[self.SCORE_KEY])), '+inf')

issue_title: How many new data points does data augmentation with mx.io.ImageRecordIter produce?
issue_body:
I think I have an understanding of the concept of data augmentation (basically you increase the dataset by transforming the given data images). I understand you can call data augmentation with mx.io.ImageRecordIter by specifying certain parameters, e.g. cropping or rotation.

However, how many 'new' data images do ImageRecordIter generate and where do you specify it? For example, does it generate just one random rotation for the training given image, or does it produce multiple rotations of the same image, say 10 rotations between +20 and -20 degrees and use the 10 'new' images to train a model?

issue_title: mismatch of padding in conv between MXNet and ONNX
issue_body:
MXNet conv currently assumes a tuple of 2 elements (height, width), while ONNX uses a tuple of 4 elements (left, right, top, bottom). Currently, when MXNet loads a ONNX model, it still uses a padding tuple of 4 elements.

The current implementation of MXNet conv doesn't check the number of elements in the tuple and just uses the first two elements in the tuple. For symmetric padding, this issue is currently ignored by MXNet.

The ideal solution should be:

- [ ]  fix the ONNX converter so that it loads the padding correctly, so MXNet always gets a padding tuple with 2 elements.
- [ ] check the parameters of the operators to make sure the inputs are correct.

@ThomasDelteil said there are some potential use cases that might require padding of (left, right, top, bottom).

@eric-haibin-lin @piiswrong @lupesko

issue_title: Dynamic Graphs
issue_body:
### Feature Request

Would be great to have dynamic unrolling of recurrent nets (LSTMs, GRUs, etc.) for training long sequences. Also to be able to implement Recursive Nets. Multidimensional Recurrent Nets (e.g. PixelRNN or PyramidLSTM) used for images require very pretty long timesteps to parse pixel by pixel. Theano scan is limited to 1D sequences and it is quite tricky to implement MDRNNs in Tensorflow. Also Memory Networks/Differential Neural Computer would benefit from dynamic graphs.

issue_title: fail with parameters of size 1, tuple issue
issue_body:
I apologize in advance for creating an issue with little documentation to support it. But I have seen this problem with a new install of version 1.0.0 as recently as a week ago.

In essence, code in gluon/parameters.py seems to choke when a parameter is of size (1,). For example, a call to net.params.get() like the following will throw an error:
`net.params.get('some_name', init= mx.init.Zero(), shape=(1,))`

In these cases, in parameters.py, self._shape is not a tuple, so subsequent calls to zip() and other functions fail. 

The fix I used was to make the following changes in gluon/parameters.py:

near line 151:
```
# the following two lines replace self._shape with a tuple
if isinstance(self._shape, tuple)==False:
    self._shape = (self._shape,)
assert len(self._shape) == len(new_shape) and \
```

similarly near line 196:
```
if isinstance(self._shape, tuple)==False:
    self._shape = (self._shape,)            
for self_dim, data_dim in zip(self.shape, data.shape):
```

similarly near line 121:
```
def __repr__(self):
    if isinstance(self._shape, tuple)==False:
        self._shape = (self._shape,)        
    s = 'Parameter {name} (shape={shape}, dtype={dtype})'
```
    
John

issue_title: Support only the 3.x release of jQuery Core
issue_body:
As of jQuery Migrate 3.0, this plugin should be paired only with Core 3.x and not older versions. That simplifies the work the plugin needs to do since it can depend on the behavior and APIs of the latest core and not have to work around all the quirks of previous versions. 

* [ ] Document this new usage in the README, which is to include the latest Core and Migrate in the old project. (Previously, we would tell users to include Migrate with the current version of Core they were using on the page.)
* [ ] Add a version check to ensure people don't misuse 3.0 by running it with older versions of jQuery.
* [x] Update Migrate code to remove any Core-version-dependent code paths, for example the [checks whether internal functions are privatized](https://github.com/jquery/jquery-migrate/blob/master/src/css.js#L4)
* [x] Determine and document the Core versions that we support for doing an upgrade. Presently it's 1.6.4 but this approach may make it possible to go back further?

issue_title: Move impyla UDF infra onto llvmlite
ironhouse.py allows default NULL connects
issue_body:
I was under the impression that when setting  `server.curve_server = True` all default NULL sessions would not be allowed to connect. In the example code below, I am using curve_server, but I am still able to inject unauthenticated data.

To reproduce:
1. Generate keys using generate_certificates.py
2. Start the modified ironshouse script below
3. inject data using  cat /dev/random |nc localhost 9000

Versions used:
* libzmq-4.0.5
* pyzmq-14.5.0
* Python-2.7.9

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python
'''
Modified version of ironhouse.
'''

import logging
import os
import sys

import zmq
import zmq.auth
from zmq.auth.thread import ThreadAuthenticator


def run():
    ''' Run Ironhouse example '''

    # These direcotries are generated by the generate_certificates script
    base_dir = os.path.dirname(__file__)
    keys_dir = os.path.join(base_dir, 'certificates')
    public_keys_dir = os.path.join(base_dir, 'public_keys')
    secret_keys_dir = os.path.join(base_dir, 'private_keys')

    if not (os.path.exists(keys_dir) and
            os.path.exists(public_keys_dir) and
            os.path.exists(secret_keys_dir)):
        logging.critical("Certificates are missing - run generate_certificates.py script first")
        sys.exit(1)

    ctx = zmq.Context().instance()

    # Start an authenticator for this context.
    auth = ThreadAuthenticator(ctx)
    auth.start()
    auth.allow('127.0.0.1')
    # Tell authenticator to use the certificate in a directory
    auth.configure_curve(domain='*', location=public_keys_dir)

    server = ctx.socket(zmq.PULL)

    server_secret_file = os.path.join(secret_keys_dir, "server.key_secret")
    server_public, server_secret = zmq.auth.load_certificate(server_secret_file)
    server.curve_secretkey = server_secret
    server.curve_publickey = server_public
    server.curve_server = True  # must come before bind
    server.bind('tcp://*:9000')

    while True:
        msg = server.recv()
        print msg

    # stop auth thread
    auth.stop()

if __name__ == '__main__':
    if zmq.zmq_version_info() < (4,0):
        raise RuntimeError("Security is not supported in libzmq version < 4.0. libzmq version {0}".format(zmq.zmq_version()))

    if '-v' in sys.argv:
        level = logging.DEBUG
    else:
        level = logging.INFO

    logging.basicConfig(level=level, format="[%(levelname)s] %(message)s")

    run()
```

from conceptmap.models.base import NLPBaseItem
from neo4j.v1 import GraphDatabase
from operator import itemgetter

class Graphaware(NLPBaseItem):
    driver = GraphDatabase.driver("bolt://localhost:7687", auth=("neo4j", "password"))


    def __init__(self, data):
        # self.lecturewise_generated_keywords = [[]] * len(data)
        self.lecturewise_generated_keywords = [[]]
        self.generated_keywords_for_each_lecture = []

        super(Graphaware, self).__init__(data=data)


    def process(self):
        self.lecturewise_generated_keywords = [[]] * len(self.data)
        lecturewise_list_of_text = self.data

        # Initialize pipeline
        Graphaware.create_pipeline()
        Graphaware.set_default_pipeline()
        Graphaware.set_fallback_lang()

        # create index for populating lecturewise_generated_keywords
        i = 0
        # select each lecture from lecturewise_list_of_text
        for list_of_text in lecturewise_list_of_text:
            # each lecture is a list of text: for ex, lec: ['text1', 'text2', 'text3', ...]
            lecture_delim  = ". "
            lecture_corpus = lecture_delim.join(list_of_text)
            # Create a node with property 'text' set to lecture_corpus
            Graphaware.add_text_node(lecture_corpus)
            # Annotate the text & generate the keywords
            Graphaware.annotate()
            Graphaware.generate_keywords()
            keywords = Graphaware.return_keywords()
            # Delete everything in the database
            Graphaware.clean_database()
            for keyword in keywords:
                self.generated_keywords_for_each_lecture.append((keyword, round(len(keyword) / 100, 3)))

            # sort generated_keywords_for_each_lecture according to scores of keyphrases
            self.generated_keywords_for_each_lecture.sort(key=itemgetter(1), reverse=True)
            # store all keywords from a lecture into an element of lecturewise_generated_keywords
            self.lecturewise_generated_keywords[i] = self.generated_keywords_for_each_lecture
            i = i + 1
            self.generated_keywords_for_each_lecture = []

        # Close the connection to the database
        Graphaware.driver.close()

    def score_keyphrases(self):
        keyphrases = self.lecturewise_generated_keywords
        print("keyphrases: {}".format(keyphrases))
        return keyphrases

    @staticmethod
    def customStopWords_present():
        """
        Check if pipeline 'customStopWords' already exists
        :return:
        """
        with Graphaware.driver.session() as session:
            result = session.run("CALL ga.nlp.processor.getPipelines")
            session.close()
            is_customstopwords_present = False
            pipeline_dict = result.data()
            num_pipelines = len(pipeline_dict)
            # print("num_pipelines: {}".format(num_pipelines))
            for i in range(0, num_pipelines):
                pipeline_i = pipeline_dict[i]
                pipeline_name = pipeline_i['name']
                if "customStopWords" == pipeline_name:
                    is_customstopwords_present = True
                    break
            return is_customstopwords_present

    @staticmethod
    def create_pipeline():
        """
        Create a pipeline
        :return:
        """
        is_customstopwords_present = Graphaware.customStopWords_present()
        if is_customstopwords_present:
            print("pipeline 'customStopWords' already exists")
        else:
            with Graphaware.driver.session() as session:
                session.run(
                "CALL ga.nlp.processor.addPipeline({textProcessor: 'com.graphaware.nlp.processor.stanford.StanfordTextProcessor', \
                name: 'customStopWords', processingSteps: {tokenize: true, ner: true, dependency: true, coref: true, relations: true, cleanxml: true, truecase: true}, \
                stopWords: '+, result, all, during', threadNumber: 20})")
                session.close()


    @staticmethod
    def set_default_pipeline():
        """
        Set a  default pipeline
        :return:
        """
        with Graphaware.driver.session() as session:
            session.run("CALL ga.nlp.processor.pipeline.default('customStopWords')")
            session.close()

    @staticmethod
    def set_fallback_lang():
        """
        Set fallback language to English
        :return:
        """
        with Graphaware.driver.session() as session:
            session.run("CALL ga.nlp.config.set('SETTING_fallbackLanguage', 'en')")
            session.close()

    @staticmethod
    def add_text_node(node_text):
        """
        Create a node with label 'Text' and set property 'text' of node to node_text
        :param node_text:
        :return:
        """
        with Graphaware.driver.session() as session:
            session.run("CREATE (n:Text {text: $node_text})", node_text=node_text)
            session.close()

    @staticmethod
    def annotate():
        """
        Annotate the text.
        The extraction is done via the annotate procedure which is the entry point to text information extraction
        :return:
        """
        with Graphaware.driver.session() as session:
            session.run("""\
            MATCH (n:Text)
            CALL ga.nlp.annotate({text: n.text, id: id(n)})
            YIELD result
            MERGE (n)-[:HAS_ANNOTATED_TEXT]->(result)
            RETURN result
            """)
            session.close()

    @staticmethod
    def generate_keywords():
        """
        Generate keywords
        :return:
        """
        with Graphaware.driver.session() as session:
            session.run("""\
            MATCH (a:AnnotatedText)
            CALL ga.nlp.ml.textRank({annotatedText: a, stopwords: '+,other,email', useDependencies: true})
            YIELD result RETURN result
            """)
            session.close()

    @staticmethod
    def return_keywords():
        with Graphaware.driver.session() as session:
            result = session.run("""\
            MATCH (n:Keyword) 
            RETURN n.originalTagValue
            """)
            session.close()
        keywords = result.data()
        keywordlist = []
        for keyword in keywords:
            keywordlist.append(keyword['n.originalTagValue'])
        return keywordlist
        # return df(result.data())

    @staticmethod
    def inspect_multiword_keyphrases():
        """
        Inspect multi-word keywords
        :return:
        """
        with Graphaware.driver.session() as session:
            result = session.run("""\
            MATCH (k:Keyword)-[:DESCRIBES]->(a:AnnotatedText)
            WHERE size(split(k.value, " "))>1
            RETURN k.value AS Keyphrase, count(*) AS n_lessons
            ORDER BY n_lessons DESC
            """)
            session.close()
            print("multi word keyphrases: {}".format(result.data()))

    @staticmethod
    def clean_database():
        """
        Delete everything in the database
        :return:
        """
        with Graphaware.driver.session() as session:
            session.run("""\
            MATCH (n)
            DETACH DELETE n
            """)
            session.close()

issue_title: Async Trait Observers
issue_body:
I'm really just throwing this idea out there to see if there's any demand for it. Perhaps this would be useful for plotting where all the rendering only occurs at the time the plot is requested? I haven't really thought about it too deeply though.

cc: @rgbkrk @SylvainCorlay @ellisonbg @minrk @tacaswell 

```python
import asyncio
import random


class AsycnTrait(object):
    
    def __set__(self, obj, value):
        task = asyncio.ensure_future(obj.handle(value))
        obj._tasks.append(task)
    
    def __get__(self, obj, cls):
        loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
        loop.run_until_complete(asyncio.gather(*obj._tasks))
        return obj._x


class HasAsyncTraits(object):
    
    a = AsycnTrait()
    
    def __init__(self):
        self._tasks = []
        self._x = 0
    
    async def handle(self, value):
        await asyncio.sleep(random.random())
        self._x = value

        
obj = HasAsyncTraits()

for i in range(10):
    obj.a = i

print(obj.a)
```

issue_title: max_memory_per_child doesn't work on Windows (with a fix)
issue_body:
Parameter `max_memory_per_child` is not passed to `Worker.__reduce__` method:

https://github.com/celery/billiard/blob/471d38e72df9cf380be90f89d0dde2735073b851/billiard/pool.py#L267-L272

As a result, on Windows, where forking is made via multi-processing, a child process doesn't receive such parameter. So, an automatic restart of a worker after reaching a memory limit doesn't happen.

To fix this issue, the method `Worker.__reduce__()` should be modified to:

```
    def __reduce__(self):
        return self.__class__, (
            self.inq, self.outq, self.synq, self.initializer,
            self.initargs, self.maxtasks, self._shutdown, self.on_exit,
            self.sigprotection, self.wrap_exception, self.max_memory_per_child
        )
```

issue_title: Documentation Error
issue_body:
Hi, 

The [documentation for Firehose](http://boto3.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/services/firehose.html#Firehose.Client.put_record) states:

```
If the PutRecord operation throws a ServiceUnavailableException , back off and retry. If the exception persists, it is possible that the throughput limits have been exceeded for the delivery stream.
```

Neither `boto3` nor `botocore` seem to actually have a `ServiceUnavailableException` to catch though! The `PutRecords` call can return an error code `ServiceUnavailable`, but since `PutRecords` can't return error codes I am at a loss as to how I can deal with exceeded throughput limits.

Can anyone help?

Cheers,
Craig

issue_body:
Hello,
I think the documentation about [create_hosted_zone](http://boto3.readthedocs.org/en/latest/reference/services/route53.html#Route53.Client.create_hosted_zone) is wrong, because if I set the attribute ```'PrivateZone'```, the request is failing, and according to [AWS API](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/Route53/latest/APIReference/API-create-hosted-zone-private.html) you don't need it.

## Steps to reproduce
with ```'PrivateZone': True```
```python
import boto3
session = boto3.session.Session(profile_name='default')

    Name='my-domain',
    VPC={
        'VPCRegion': 'my-region',

    HostedZoneConfig={

)

issue_title: improve listing of catalog items
Add clear error message if invalid mode is configured
issue_body:
### General information

* SDK/Library version: 1.12.0
* Environment: Any (see description)
* Python: 2.7.10

### Issue description

Currently, it is possible to pass any gibberish to `paypalrestsdk.configure` as `mode`, and it will happily accept it. Creating payment with such gibberish mode doesn't work, obviously.

Minimal example:

    paypalrestsdk.configure({
        "mode": "gibberish mode",  # offending line
        "client_id": settings.PAYPAL_CLIENT_ID,
        "client_secret": settings.PAYPAL_CLIENT_SECRET
    })
    payment_info = {
        "intent": "sale",
        "payer": {"payment_method": "paypal"},
        "transactions": [{
            "item_list": {"items": [{"name": "item",
                                              "sku": "item",
                                              "price": "1.00",
                                              "currency": "USD",
                                              "quantity": 1}]},
            "amount": {"total": "1.00",
                              "currency": "USD"},
        }],
        "redirect_urls": {"return_url": '/',
                                  "cancel_url": '/cancel/'}
    }
    payment = paypalrestsdk.Payment(payment_info)
    payment.create()

The code above yields a following error:

    Traceback (most recent call last):
    File "/projects/paypal_example.py", line 36, in <module>
        payment.create()
    File "/python_env/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/paypalrestsdk/resource.py", line 163, in create
        new_attributes = self.api.post(self.path, self.to_dict(), headers, refresh_token)
    File "/python_env/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/paypalrestsdk/api.py", line 272, in post
        return self.request(util.join_url(self.endpoint, action), 'POST', body=params or {}, headers=headers or {}, refresh_token=refresh_token)
    File "/python_env/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/paypalrestsdk/util.py", line 22, in join_url
        url = re.sub(r'/?$', re.sub(r'^/?', '/', path), url)
    File "/python_env/lib/python2.7/re.py", line 155, in sub
        return _compile(pattern, flags).sub(repl, string, count)
    TypeError: expected string or buffer

As I understand, in this case `Api` object uses default endpoint from `paypalrestsdk.config.__endpoint_map__`, but uses `.get`, so incorrect mode yields `None`.

I think it would be better to fail earlier, say, in `Api.__init__`, and throw something like

    ValueError: incorrect mode; expected "sandbox" or "live"

instead of spilling internals like that.

I understand that docs explicitly specify that only "sandbox" or "live" settings can be used, but readable error message is always an improvement.

issue_title: RATE LIMIT REACHED
Open source s2protocol (+mpq parser) implementation in Java and Go
issue_body:
Hello Everyone,

I'd like to announce that I have implemented / ported the `s2protocol` library to **Java** and **Go** languages. They are available as open-source projects on Github.

Java implementation is part of the open source [Scelight](https://github.com/icza/scelight) project (which is much more than just a replay parser, it also gives a high-level api):

**Java MPQ parser:** https://github.com/icza/scelight/tree/master/src-app-libs/hu/belicza/andras/mpq
**Java s2protocol:** https://github.com/icza/scelight/tree/master/src-app/hu/scelight/sc2/rep/s2prot

Go implementation is a standalone project for both MPQ and s2protocol implementation:

**Go MPQ parser:** https://github.com/icza/mpq
**Go s2protocol:** https://github.com/icza/s2prot

The Go implementation is fairly new and will improve over time.

Hope this helps others. Should you have any questions regarding these libraries, I'd be happy to answer them.

Have fun coding and parsing replays. Don't forget to star / watch if you like them.

issue_title: Wildcard argument matching support
issue_body:
This is a feature request for the ability to support extensions to argument matching behavior.

For example, the Java mocking library [mockito](https://mockito.googlecode.com/hg-history/1.6/javadoc/org/mockito/Matchers.html) supports a wide array of argument matchers. This reduces coupling between the test and parameters irrelevant to the test.

Case in point:

```
    allow(tchannel).thrift(
        UserGateway.userstore_client.getUser(userUuid=str(user_uuid)),
        timeout=config.get('populous.timeout')
    ).and_return_future(
        mock.Mock(body=T)
    )
```

In this test, I don't really care what the timeout is. But to mock correctly, I still have to specify it. 

Similarly, mock also has wildcard support in the form of mock.ANY etc.

issue_title: cryptography should be a test dependency
issue_body:
The `cryptography` package is never imported by the `nexmo` package; it is only imported by `test_nexmo.py`. Therefore it should be included in `setup.py` in `tests_require`[1] rather than `install_requires`. That way users who just want to use `nexmo` without running its tests won't end up unnecessarily installing `cryptography` when they `pip install nexmo`. This is especially important because installing cryptography often requires a C compiler, which may not be available in some environments, this also takes a lot longer, bloats their environment with unneeded dependencies, etc. (You can also add a `test` entry under `extras_require`[2] so that users who do want to install the test dependencies can run `pip install nexmo [test]`.)

[1] http://python-packaging.readthedocs.io/en/latest/testing.html
[2] https://stackoverflow.com/questions/30239152/specify-extras-require-with-pip-install-e

issue_title: Move unit and acceptance test to own folders
issue_body:
The current test folder structure is little bit messy and makes writing the instructions somewhat difficult. Also the test runner could be enhanced to better support PyPy and Jython. 
- [ ] Move unit and acceptance test to individual folders in the project root folder, like `utest` and `atest`
- [ ] When running acceptance tests, it is still good idea to run unit test first.
- [ ] Check that unit test runner to pick up the interpreter correctly, example `pypy test/run_unit_tests.py` must use pypy to run the unit tests and not the system default python. 
- [ ] Fix acceptance test runner, to pick up the interpreter correctly from the command line. Example `pypy test/run_tests.py` should run unit and acceptance test by using pypy and not by OS default Python.

This is not mandatory to implement for release 3.0.0 and can be scoped to be done also after the 3.0.0 release.

I have successfully printed the data in C++ with something like `double x = grip->GetNumberArray("ball/x", llvm::ArrayRef<double>())` (where `grip` is the network table and `ball` is a subtable).  However, it doesn't seem to work in Python:

Is there anything else I should try?  I confirmed that the data is in network tables with the TableViewer utility.

issue_title: Moving/renaming project home
issue_body:
If you move the project home it leaves the virtualenv folder. This is a problem if you create a new project home with the same name/path because it will use the virtualenv folder with the packages of the other project. 

##### Describe your environment

1. Ubuntu
1. Python version: Python 2.7.6
1. Pipenv version: pipenv, version 9.0.0

##### Expected result

Keep the virtualenv when you move/rename the project home. A new virtualenv when you create a new project in the same path as an older project.
That is, move virtualenv folder when you move/rename the project home.

##### Actual result

After moving/renaming the project home pipenv creates a new virtualenv and it has to reinstall all packages again. The old virtualenv remains there wasting space.
Creating a new project in the same path keeps the older project packages.

issue_title: run build_clib before build_ext in develop command?
issue_body:
The brotlipy project has a CFFI extension module that links to a static C library compiled using `build_clib` command.

When using the setuptools `develop` command (or `pip install -e`), the linkage with `libbrotli` fails because `develop` only calls `build_ext` without ensuring that `build_clib` has been called.

See https://github.com/pypa/pip/issues/4523

One workaround is to make a `build_ext` subclass that always runs `build_clib` before running itself:
https://github.com/python-hyper/brotlipy/pull/92

I wonder if this behavior should be the default?

thanks

issue_title: Move the default agent behavior to HTTPClient

issue_title: Connecting to any data source throwing error - Attribute Error : Field object has no attribute 'description'
build_docs swallows errors b/c of changed code in Sphinx
issue_body:
When there is an error in the Sphinx build, fails in astropy-helpers' build_docs command fails with 
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 39, in <module>
NameError: name 'self' is not defined
Sphinx Documentation subprocess failed with return code 1
```

This is because astropy injects the run method of build_docs into a subprocess, but doesn't inject the command object itself. The command object (``self``) is referenced in [line 190 of sphinx/setup_command.py](https://github.com/sphinx-doc/sphinx/blob/master/sphinx/setup_command.py#L190). The code generated for the subprocess is

```
from __future__ import print_function
from sphinx.setup_command import *

os.chdir(u'/Users/alexrudy/Dropbox/Library/Development/Astronomy/Zeeko/docs')
sys.path.insert(0, u'/Users/alexrudy/Dropbox/Library/Development/Astronomy/Zeeko/build/lib.macosx-10.11-x86_64-2.7')
sys.path.insert(0, '/Users/alexrudy/Dropbox/Library/Development/Astronomy/Zeeko/astropy_helpers')

if not color_terminal():
    nocolor()
if not 3:
    status_stream = StringIO()
else:
    status_stream = sys.stdout
confoverrides = {}
if '':
    confoverrides['project'] = ''
if '':
    confoverrides['version'] = ''
if '':
    confoverrides['release'] = ''
if '':
    confoverrides['today'] = ''
if '':
    confoverrides['copyright'] = ''

app = None
try:
    with docutils_namespace():
        app = Sphinx(u'./docs', u'./docs',
                     u'./build/sphinx/html', u'./build/sphinx/doctrees',
                     'html', confoverrides, status_stream,
                     freshenv=False,
                     warningiserror=False)
        app.build(force_all=False)
        if app.statuscode:
            raise DistutilsExecError(
                'caused by %s builder.' % app.builder.name)
except Exception as exc:
    handle_exception(app, self, exc, sys.stderr)
    if not False:
        raise SystemExit(1)

if False:
    src = app.config.master_doc + app.builder.out_suffix
    dst = app.builder.get_outfilename('index')
    os.symlink(src, dst)

```

I'm not sure what to do about this. Maybe pickle the command object? That seems bad, and swallowing the error is really frustrating.

issue_title: Segfaults from not-initialized pointer in skimage/measure/_ccomp.pyx
issue_body:
See analysis at: https://bugs.debian.org/cgi-bin/bugreport.cgi?bug=840589#35

Quoting from there:

```
10 minutes of debugging on *i386* (not a fancy architecture) with GDB
shows that the problem is actually not limited to some unsupported
upstream architectures, but rather that it only work by chance on
architectures which pass the test. The code is buggy and will return
at best wrong values on amd64.

From skimage/measure/_ccomp.pyx:

| cdef void scanBG(DTYPE_t *data_p, DTYPE_t *forest_p, shape_info *shapeinfo,
|                  bginfo *bg):
|     """
|     Settle all background pixels now and don't bother with them later.
|     Since this only requires one linar sweep through the array, it is fast
|     and it makes sense to do it separately.
| 
|     The result of this function is update of forest_p and bg parameter.
|     """
|     cdef DTYPE_t i, bgval = bg.background_val, firstbg
|     # We find the provisional label of the background, which is the index of
|     # the first background pixel
|     for i in range(shapeinfo.numels):
|         if data_p[i] == bgval:
|             firstbg = i
|             bg.background_node = firstbg
|             break
| 
|     # And then we apply this provisional label to the whole background
|     for i in range(firstbg, shapeinfo.numels):
|         if data_p[i] == bgval:
|             forest_p[i] = firstbg

As you can see above, the variable firstbg is only initialized
conditionally in the first loop. It depends on the array values and the
and background value. Still it is used as the starting pointer on the
second loop, even if uninitialized. At best the pointer point to the
wrong address, at worst it generates an out of bound access causing the
segmentation fault.

The test uses the following array with bg.background_val = -1

|  x = np.array([[1, 0, 6],
|                [0, 0, 6],
|                [5, 5, 5]])

The problem has been introduced in the following upstream commit:

| commit d29831882daef9b7d7691becc3669349d642b547
| Author: Matěj Týč <matej.tyc@gmail.com>
| Date:   Sat Nov 15 16:42:22 2014 +0100
| 
|     Redone the background handling = considerable speedup
```

This may well be the cause of the segfaults on 32-bit reported over at #2111

issue_body:

issue_title: Implement SDR classifier in nupic.core
issue_body: This task is to implement sdr_classifier.cpp in nupic.core for better efficiency. The C++ version should give the same result as https://github.com/numenta/nupic/issues/3053

issue_title: Investigate optimizations on C++ TM code
I don't have a way to visualize Connections events
issue_body:
The 4 green charts on this page are essentially not possible with the C++ TemporalMemory: http://mrcslws.com/blocks/2016/02/15/htm-stacks-of-time-series.html

In Python I do that by patching the Connections class at runtime, replacing its methods with my own wrappers. This won't work in C++. In C++ we'd either have to make the TemporalMemory's connections implementation pluggable, or add event handlers to the Connections class.

I like event handlers.

issue_title: In Bamboo build, distutils uses Visual C++ instead of the mingw32 toolchain, despite distutils.cfg
issue_body:
ci/bamboo/build-windows.ps1 in https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/pull/1076 copies external\windows64-gcc\bin\distutils.cfg to $($env:PYTHONHOME)\Lib\distutils.cfg. This distutils.cfg instructs distutils to use the mingw32 toolchain.

The same logic works as desired in nupic.core's AppVeyor build, resulting in the use of mingw to build the dummy.c file when generating bdist_wheel. However, in the Bamboo build, it invokes Visual C++ instead.

issue_title: setup fails when LC_ALL=C is set
issue_body:
I believe the issue here is that when LC_ALL=C is set the encoding is switched to `encodings/ascii.py` and we get an error which is `UnicodeDecodeError: 'ascii' codec can't decode byte 0xc3 in position 880: ordinal not in range(128)`. Here is the error trace:

```
File "/var/www/venv/eis/build/django-chartjs/setup.py", line 19, in <module>
long_description=read_relative_file('README.rst'),
File "/var/www/venv/eis/build/django-chartjs/setup.py", line 11, in read_relative_file
return f.read()
File "/var/www/venv/eis/lib/python3.4/encodings/ascii.py", line 26, in decode
return codecs.ascii_decode(input, self.errors)[0]
UnicodeDecodeError: 'ascii' codec can't decode byte 0xc3 in position 880: ordinal not in range(128)
```

Because ascii does not support some symbols used in the html which is the part of this README.rst we get the error. This is not an issue in the most of cases but makes impossible to install the module via some provisioning systems like SaltStack.

issue_title: spires parser should special-case journals
issue_body:
Originally on 2011-08-30

journal shouldn't display the behavior that most other things do, where they say that:
find t foo bar baz
means:
title:foo and title:bar and title:baz

it's not obvious, because of search_unit parsing of comma splits and so forth, but I think what we want is more like:
find j nucl phys b
becomes:
journal:"nucl phys b"

issue_title: accounts: userEXT id as VARBINARY
issue_body:
In the userEXT table, the ```id``` is created as ```db.VARBINARY```

https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio/blob/master/invenio/modules/accounts/models.py#L426

That seems to be the only occurrence of ```db.VARBINARY``` in Invenio and does not make much sense as that column holds ```Strings```. I just realised about it because I am having problems interacting with that column with an external MySQL editor like MySQL Workbench.

Would it make sense to change it to ```db.String``` ?

issue_title: errors: include Sentry-ID in error response payload
issue_labels: [{'id': 473693023, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/inveniosoftware/invenio-rest/labels/Service:%20ZENODO', 'name': 'Service: ZENODO', 'color': '207de5', 'default': False}, {'id': 414895604, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/inveniosoftware/invenio-rest/labels/Size:%20beginner-friendly', 'name': 'Size: beginner-friendly', 'color': '8646c6', 'default': False}, {'id': 279113239, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/inveniosoftware/invenio-rest/labels/Type:%20enhancement', 'name': 'Type: enhancement', 'color': 'ff5500', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
REST API error responses, for instances that log exceptions to Sentry, could benefit from including the Sentry Event ID in the payload. Maybe the best place would be [here](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-rest/blob/1cf6be1bb97e57000780246f9a6814d0b3593836/invenio_rest/views.py#L54), with something like:

```python
if hasattr(g, 'sentry_event_id'):
    kwargs['internal_error_id'] = g.sentry_event_id
```

issue_title: cli: npm command should generated valid version numbers in package.json
issue_body:
When bootstrapping a new Invenio instance, the version in the package is set to ``0.1.0.dev0``. This version is also used by the ``npm`` command when generating the ``package.json``:

```json
{
    "name": "mysite",
    "version": "0.1.0.dev0",
    "dependencies": {

issue_title: alembic: cannot drop tables after alembic upgrade heads
issue_body:
The problem occurs when running the example app in invenio-accounts, adding:
```
app.config['SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI'] = os.environ.get(
    'SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI','postgresql+psycopg2://postgres@localhost:5432/invenio'
)
```

in order to use postgresql, and executing the following:
`flask db init`
`flask alembic upgrade heads`
`flask db drop`

The stacktrace:
```sqlalchemy.exc.InternalError: (psycopg2.InternalError) cannot drop table accounts_user because other objects depend on it
DETAIL:  constraint fk_transaction_accounts_user_id on table transaction depends on table accounts_user
HINT:  Use DROP ... CASCADE to drop the dependent objects too.
 [SQL: '\nDROP TABLE accounts_user']
```

This is due to the alteration of the transaction table [here](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-accounts/blob/master/invenio_accounts/alembic/9848d0149abd_create_accounts_tables.py#L93) that doesn't include a cascade delete for the foreign key fk_transaction_accounts_user_id.

I'm not sure if this way of dropping the tables should be used after creating with alembic, and in this case a warning message could be added.

issue_title: loadBefore raises KeyError instead of POSKeyError?
issue_body:
I noticed that TemporaryStorage raises KeyError in loadBefore: 
https://github.com/zopefoundation/tempstorage/blob/master/src/tempstorage/TemporaryStorage.py#L187

But FileStorage raises POSKeyError for the same purpose (AFAIK): https://github.com/zopefoundation/ZODB/blob/master/src/ZODB/FileStorage/FileStorage.py#L562

Shouldn't the KeyError in TemporaryStorage be a POSKeyError as well?

The thing is that when using collective.recipe.plonesite with a ZEO setup and a temp_folder on the zeoserver I see the following traceback:

```

    return self.storage.loadBefore(oid, tid)

issue_title: fire.Fire() ruins fire.Fire(fire.Fire)
issue_body:
Hi python-fire,

`fire.Fire` looked like a very interesting function so I wanted to inspect it with `fire.Fire`. I was surprised that the interpreter printed out a dictionary with all my locals and globals.

I'm not sure if that's what you guys intended to happen, I guess it kind of is because of `fire.Fire()` defined as a valid invocation which means `fire.Fire(globals() + locals())`. Perhaps it's worth considering introducing a different function that would do that? `fire.FireThisModule()`?

issue_title: Useful local variables in *stack* frames are removed
issue_body:
When using `auto_log_stacks=True`, frames are pruned by `slim_frame_data` and only local-variables of top/bottom 12 frames are kept. This is because `in_app` are not calculated for stack frames and `include_paths` are ignored.

When using `auto_log_stacks` bottom frames are usually in `raven` or `logging` package, and top frames are usually framework frames (like uwsgi or django), and this results in loss of useful information.

Also it's a good idea to add configuration to change the `frame_allowance`, this is hard-coded to 25 in the code and is not configurable.

issue_title: xsd:base64Binary can't be converted to a string
issue_body:
The XSDToPython conversion function in term.py tries to decode base64 literals and treat them as python strings.  This doesn't work when the encoded data is (or happens to resemble) a different character encoding. 

Example:

```
>>> ttl1 = """
@prefix fhir: <http://hl7.org/fhir/> .
@prefix xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#> .

<http://hl7.org/fhir/Binary/example> fhir:value "JVBERi0xLjUNJeLjz9MNCjEwIDAgb2JqDTw8L0xpbmVhcml6ZWQgMS9MIDEzMDA2OC9PIDEyL0Ug MTI1NzM1L04gMS9UIDEyOTc2NC9IIFsgNTQ2IDIwNF0+Pg1lbmRvYmoNICAgICAgICAgICAg"^^xsd:base64Binary . """
>>> rdflib.Graph().parse(data=ttl1, format="turtle")
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
      ...
File "/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.5/lib/python3.5/site-packages/rdflib/term.py", line 589, in __new__
    lexical_or_value = lexical_or_value.decode('utf-8')
UnicodeDecodeError: 'utf-8' codec can't decode byte 0xe2 in position 10: invalid continuation byte
>>>
```

What is happening is that  the XSDToPython table in term.py (~1482) converts base64Binary to bytes:

```
>>> x = base64.b64decode("JVBERi0xLjUNJeLjz9MNCjEwIDAgb2JqDTw8L0xpbmVhcml6ZWQgMS9MIDEzMDA2OC9PIDEyL0Ug MTI1NzM1L04gMS9UIDEyOTc2NC9IIFsgNTQ2IDIwNF0+Pg1lbmRvYmoNICAgICAgICAgICAg") 
>>> x
b'%PDF-1.5\r%\xe2\xe3\xcf\xd3\r\n10 0 obj\r<</Linearized 1/L 130068/O 12/E 125735/N 1/T 129764/H [ 546 204]>>\rendobj\r            '
```

term.py then attempts to convert a bytes value into a string assuming that the original data was some sort of utf-8 string to begin with(!!!)

```
    if py3compat.PY3 and isinstance(lexical_or_value, bytes):
        lexical_or_value = lexical_or_value.decode('utf-8')
```

This doesn't work.  In the above example, x is latin1 encoded.  

```
>>> x.decode('utf-8')
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
UnicodeDecodeError: 'utf-8' codec can't decode byte 0xe2 in position 10: invalid continuation byte
```

**If** you were able to determine that it is, in fact, an encoded string, you could solve this issue with:

```
>>> x.decode('latin1')
'%PDF-1.5\r%âãÏÓ\r\n10 0 obj\r<</Linearized 1/L 130068/O 12/E 125735/N 1/T 129764/H [ 546 204]>>\rendobj\r            '
```

But you can't.  Take another example - I want to encode some arbitrary binary data:

```
>>> binary_data = bytes([0, 17, 243, 117, 2])
>>> binary_data
b'\x00\x11\xf3u\x02'
```

Encode it as a base64 literal, which I can place in an arbitrary block of RDF

```
>>> base64.b64encode(binary_data).decode('utf-8')
'ABHzdQI=''
>>> ttl2 = """@prefix ex: <http://example.org/> .
 @prefix xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#> .

 ex:foo ex:v "ABHzdQI="^^xsd:base64Binary ."""
>>> rdflib.Graph().parse(data=ttl2, format="turtle")
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
       ...
File "/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.5/lib/python3.5/site-packages/rdflib/term.py", line 589, in __new__
    lexical_or_value = lexical_or_value.decode('utf-8')
UnicodeDecodeError: 'utf-8' codec can't decode byte 0xf3 in position 2: invalid continuation byte
```

---

Recommendation:  rdflib Graph parsers should leave base64binary literals alone.  The user needs to understand the context in order to convert them into anything else (that is why they are binary to begin with...)

Replace line 1465 and 1466:

```
     URIRef(
        _XSD_PFX + 'base64Binary'): lambda s: base64.b64decode(py3compat.b(s)),
```

with

```
     URIRef(_XSD_PFX + 'base64Binary') : None,
```

issue_title: galsim.lsst module is broken

The galsim.lsst unit tests are skipped if the lsst stack is not setup, which is usually the case when I'm running the galsim unit tests.  I decided recently though to try these tests out again, and found that the stack has apparently evolved significantly since the galsim.lsst module was written.

To start, the lsst.pex.logging package has more or less been removed since Jan '17 (https://jira.lsstcorp.org/browse/RFC-29), and the cameraGeom.PUPIL coordinate system was renamed cameraGeom.FIELD_ANGLE around August '17 (https://community.lsst.org/t/new-camera-geometry-based-on-transform/2188). issue_title: 3D disk galaxy models
issue_body:
For some Euclid simulations, the group at Oxford (Lance Miller, Niraj Welikala) has been building 3D bulge+disk galaxies.  The bulge can be represented by GalSim already, but the 3D disk cannot be.  They model it as a disk of finite thickness when viewed edge-on, with a sech^2(z) surface brightness distribution as observed for real edge-on galaxies (the radial part of the profile is indeed exponential as you move along the disk at fixed z, so it's the sech^2(z) aspect that would be new to GalSim). Initially they did this via photon-shooting but they recently began representing it using DFTs. Lance has offered some C code that could be used to implement the 3D disk models in GalSim.  He does not plan to implement it in GalSim himself.

Unlike many of our profiles, the input parameter would be the disk inclination, with the ellipticity being a derived parameter.   I'm mentioning this not as a problem, but rather as an interesting feature.

Any thoughts, questions, concerns, etc.?

issue_title: Icon request - Coins icon
issue_body:
I know that you have a `money` icon (plus some currency icons), but might be good to have a `coins` icon too (e.g. https://www.google.com/search?q=coins+icon).
I don't quite understand immediately that this : http://fortawesome.github.io/Font-Awesome/icon/money/
...is a bill although a green color might help Americans figure it out.

But I am not a graphics guy, so perhaps two coins will be harder to recognize.
So, it's up to you.
I only wanted to throw the idea.

----

Also, I was always wondering if there is a way to pick and choose which icons will be included in your production server to reduce the bandwidth. Certainly this could be done with CSS files manually, but I don't know if it is possible with all of the `image` data for the icons.
If it is possible, let me know. :)
(The format of the EOT/WOFF/TTF/etc files looks quite frightening to decode at first glance...)
I might make an EXE that scans a directory's files to find all used `fa-*`s and build a reduced in size FA library.

I think that would be cool.
I guess that the fact that your library has so many icons can be both a good thing (in terms of choices) and a bad (in terms of performance). This process might be great to get rid of all the bad things. :)

A quick search looks promising: http://stackoverflow.com/questions/4628247/c-sharp-lib-for-processing-font-files-ttf-truetype-others

issue_title: format id fails when no results are found with schedule list
issue_body:
When searching with schedule list and format human if no results are found that is returned to the user and the exit code is 0
```
$ tower-cli schedule list -Q description 43 
No records found.
$ echo $?
0
```
If you search for an object with format id however an error is generated and the process exits as a failure
```
$ tower-cli schedule list -Q description 43 -f id
Error: Can not use id format when multiple objects are returned.
$ echo $?
62
```

For my use case exit 0 with no output seems correct. The program was able to perform the query against the tower server and it does list all matching ids, there just don't happen to be any. Either way this seems like a bug.

issue_title: Security for API
issue_body:
Currently the API is completely unsecured.  This limits us adding new features to the API which could actually destroy or reveal data, and isn't a great idea in general.  As such, I'd like two things for the API:

* simple user/password auth
* SSL support

There's two ways we could approach this: one would be to add these things using various snippets to the existing API.py (for example: https://gist.github.com/fxsjy/5465353).  The second way would be to offer sample configurations for an nginx proxy for the API.

Thoughts?

issue_title: Default parameters break with collectionFormat csv
issue_body:
### Description

Can no longer define a `default` value for a query parameter with `type: array`, `items: {type: string}` and `collectionFormat: csv`. Possibly this is because of a change in swagger-spec-validator.

### Expected behaviour

In connexion version 1.3 (and swagger-spec-validator 2.1.0), a string default value worked in the above case.
An array value would be fine too, and be more consistent with the `collectionFormat`.

### Actual behaviour

With a string default, eg. ```default: foobar```,
```
swagger_spec_validator.common.SwaggerValidationError: ("'foobar' is not of type 'array'\n\nFailed validating 'type' in schema:\n    {'collectionFormat': 'csv',\n     'default': 'foobar',\n     'in': 'query',\n     'items': {'type': 'string'},\n     'name': 'bar',\n     'type': 'array'}\n\nOn instance:\n    'foobar'", <ValidationError: "'foobar' is not of type 'array'">)
```
With an array default, eg. ```default: ["foobar"]```
```
  File ".../connexion/decorators/validation.py", line 55, in validate_type
    parts = value.split(",")
AttributeError: 'list' object has no attribute 'split'
```

### Steps to reproduce

Use this `swagger.yaml` file: 
```
swagger: "2.0"
info:
  title: Thing
  version: "0"
paths:
  /foo:
    get:
      operationId: "test_app.api.foo"
      parameters:
        - $ref: "#/parameters/bar"
      responses:
        200:
          description: "Results."

parameters:
  bar:
    name: bar
    in: query
    type: array
    items:
      type: string
    collectionFormat: csv
    default: foobar
```
Use this `api.py`:
```
import connexion
def foo(bar):
  return bar
```
then execute:
```
import os
swagger_path = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(os.path.realpath(__file__)), 'swagger.yaml')
app.add_api(swagger_path, base_path='/v0', strict_validation=True, validate_responses=False)
app.run()
```

### Additional info:

Output of the commands:

- `python --version`
Python 3.6.5 :: Anaconda, Inc.
- `pip show connexion | grep "^Version\:"`
Version: 1.4.2

Also using swagger-spec-validator version 2.3.1.

issue_title: Get tab index of the running command
issue_body:
How can I get the tab index of a long running command which is not focused ?

`guake -g` returns the index of the currently focused tab, not the tab in which `guake -g` is running.

For example, run :
```shell
guake -g
```

Then, run :
```shell
sleep 5; guake -g
```

And switch tab before `guake -g` is triggered to see that returned indexes are different.

issue_title: [gtk3] Opens terminal by default
issue_body:
In the gtk2 version, the command "guake" only presented a desktop notification about F12.

In the gtk3 version, it also opens the terminal.

I'm not a guake user myself, so it's hard for me to imagine, but I expect it's the kind of terminal emulator that you start up (I mean you start up the process listening for F12) when the desktop starts, and then invoke when required. So probably the former behavior makes more sense.

Anyway, opening up a terminal straight away doesn't play nicely along with the message "press F12 to use it". I mean it's already there and usable, and F12 hides it, so why shall I press F12 to use it?? If this new behavior is the new preferred one, maybe consider using different wording such as "press F12 to toggle its window" or alike...?

issue_title: Make parameters node accessible through the Web API
issue_body:
In OpenFisca, the structure of parameters is a tree. For instance:
- `prestations.minima_sociaux.rsa.montant_de_base_du_rsa` is a leaf that represents a concrete parameter of the legislation
- `prestations.minima_sociaux.rsa` is a node that represents a structured group of parameters of the legislation

Today, in the Web API, I can access two things: 
- A [leaf](https://fr.openfisca.org/api/v21/parameter/prestations.minima_sociaux.rsa.montant_de_base_du_rsa).
- The [flattened list of all leafs](https://fr.openfisca.org/api/v21/parameters).

I'm trying to represent, in a web view, a node of the legislation parameters.
But there is no way for me to directly access a node of the parameters.
The best I can do is parse the list of leafs to reconstruct the graph, but this seems silly and tedious, and it doesn't allow me to access the metadata associated with a node (description).

-------

As a client of the API building a front displaying the parameters,
I'd like to be able to access a node through the Web API,
So that I can build a view of this node that include its metadata

This last function returns sometimes the `NaN`, in particular [this line](https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-core/blob/master/openfisca_core/taxscales.py#L198)

Should we add a check here? or is the problem upstream?

Here is the context:

```

issue_title: What do you think about making it more flexible?
issue_body:
This is an awesome project! Congrats! It'a amazing how complete it is.

For me it would be nice to be able to use the queryinspect programatically instead of coupling it to my project and making it depending on settings.

I teach a lot and work on many projects that need refactoring and performance improvements. So I think it would be great to use the inspector without the middleware.

I have some rudimentary solution for what I need with the `logdb` function fo [sqlformatter](https://github.com/henriquebastos/sqlformatter). [You can see the source at here](https://github.com/henriquebastos/sqlformatter/blob/master/sqlformatter.py).

Be decoupling the inspector from the predetermined workflow of a middleware one could:

- use as a view decorator
- use as a contextmanager
- turn it on on the beginning of a process and off somewhere else.
- easily integrate with other tools like django-debugtoolbar and django-devserver

If you feel this is aligned with your vision, I can do some work on that.

Again, congrats and thanks for sharing.

issue_title: google-cloud-trace wheels are **not** universal
issue_body:
This is because of a missing `trace/setup.cfg`. For example, in the latest [release][1] the file uploaded corresponds to the Python that built the wheel: `google_cloud_trace-0.16.0-py3-none-any.whl` (it should end with `-py2.py3-none-any.whl`.

@lukesneeringer I am assigning to you because I think this is a codegen issue. There is a simple fix to add a two line file `setup.cfg`:

```
[bdist_wheel]
universal = 1
```

[1]: https://pypi.org/project/google-cloud-trace/0.16.0/

issue_title: Support for Bucket.location setter
issue_body:
I was trying to change location of the bucket in Storage, but everything gets updated apart from location. It looks like the data is being sent through API, but are not persisted?

Code example
```python
storage_client = StorageClient()
bucket = storage_client.create_bucket(name)
bucket.storage_class = 'MULTI_REGIONAL'
bucket.location = 'EU' # tried 'eu' as well
bucket.labels = {
     'project': 'etl-transformer',
     'group': 'etl',
}
bucket.patch()
```

```python
# update_properties printed in _PropertyMixin:patch
{
    'labels': {
        'project': 'etl-transformer',
        'group': 'etl'
    },
    'storageClass': 'MULTI_REGIONAL',
    'location': 'EU'
}

# I checked here even dump in requests package to be sure it's being passed to the http object

# api_reponse
{
    u 'labels': {
      u 'project': u 'etl-transformer',
      u 'group': u 'etl'
    },
    u 'location': u 'US',
    u 'storageClass': u 'MULTI_REGIONAL'
}
```

I know that documentation doesn't say that `location` attribute is a setter, but under the hood it's a `_scalar_property` which is appended to changed properties and according to json API, location is writable:

https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/json_api/v1/buckets

issue_title: BigQuery: doesn't declare dependency on concurrent.futures
issue_body:
[bigquery/client.py imports concurrent.futures](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/09897f31f83023ae9059a0eb4296b15d4785678d/bigquery/google/cloud/bigquery/client.py#L20) but it doesn't declare the dependency in [setup.py](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/09897f31f83023ae9059a0eb4296b15d4785678d/bigquery/setup.py#L53). It should have a declaration like [the one from google-cloud-core==0.27.1](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/bee32c0ae68b1e6cf224f9017c758a8e31e8be83/core/setup.py#L63) (removed in 0.28.0).

Currently this is doesn't break anything because [api-core depends on futures](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/09897f31f83023ae9059a0eb4296b15d4785678d/api_core/setup.py#L62), but if that dependency disappears it will break.

This may be true for other clients, I haven't checked. I ran into it when looking at bazelbuild/rules_python#14.

issue_title: Error Reporting: Docs for HTTPContext incorrect
issue_body:
The [documentation](https://googlecloudplatform.github.io/google-cloud-python/latest/error-reporting/usage.html) for `error_reporting.client.HTTPContext` gives the wrong parameter names.

```python
>>> from google.cloud import error_reporting
>>> client = error_reporting.Client()
>>> user = 'example@gmail.com'
>>> http_context = HTTPContext(method='GET', url='/', userAgent='test agent',
...                            referrer='example.com', responseStatusCode=500,
...                            remote_ip='1.2.3.4')
>>> try:
>>>     raise NameError
>>> except Exception:
>>>     client.report_exception(http_context=http_context, user=user))
```

Reading the code, it's clear that the camel-cased names are internally used to match expected JSON keys for the underlying API call, but the [actual parameter names are properly snake-cased](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/master/error_reporting/google/cloud/error_reporting/client.py#L64-L74). Oddly, the docs _do_ show the correct parameter name for `remote_ip`, but not `user_agent` or `response_status_code`.

I couldn't figure out which file to edit to fix, otherwise I would have just submitted a PR.

issue_title: Crawler.crawl_remote returns incorrect results if index is redirected
issue_body:
The PyPI-compatible server [devpi](http://doc.devpi.net/latest/) will issue redirects to the lowercased package page if an uppercased package name (e.g. `Django`) is searched. For example, if the index URL is `https://HOST/wyatt/test/+simple/` (with or without trailing slash), `pex` will query `https://HOST/wyatt/test/+simple/Django/` to install requirement `Django==1.6.5`. devpi will redirect to `https://HOST/wyatt/test/+simple/django` (_without_ a trailing slash) and the crawler crawls URLs on the index page (presumably because `requests` follows the redirect), the links in which are relative to the URL without the trailing slash (i.e. are one level shallower with respect to leading `..` segments).

In `pex.crawler.Crawler.crawl_remote`, the links are joined to the original URL and the link object's URL isn't updated to reflect the new canonical URL of the page. This results in incorrect resolution of the relative URLs on the index since the trailing slash is missing.

Pip itself doesn't have this issue with devpi.

Anyway, I think we can work around this on our end by slapping on a trailing slash with NGINX, but I think for the sake of correctness this should be updated in Pex itself. Happy to work on a patch if this is a valid issue.

The pex version this happens in is 1.1.2.

issue_title: JVM subsystem options are invisible in task help
issue_body:
In pants 0.0.39, some changes to the option subsystem have landed that enable some cool things, like using options in the JVM subsystem anywhere (cool!). However, these options are invisible in the help, unless `--help-advanced` is specified. There are two issues with this -- `--help-advanced` contains a ton of options now, so it's hard to find things. And secondly, I don't want to tell users to use `--help-advanced` -- the whole point of that is to hide things from the general populace, that are only necessary for pants admins.

For example, when trying to enable debugging for junit tests. Compare this:

```
[prometheus source2 (master)]$ ./pants test -h |grep debug
```

To this:

```
[prometheus source2 (master)]$ ./pants test -h --help-advanced|grep debug
--[no-]jvm-test-junit-debug (default: None)
    Run the JVM with remote debugging.
--jvm-test-junit-debug-port=<int> (default: 5005)
    The JVM will listen for a debugger on this port.
--jvm-test-junit-debug-args="['str1','str2',...]" (default: ['-Xdebug','-Xrunjdwp:transport=dt_socket,server=y,suspend=y,address={debug_port}'])
    The JVM remote-debugging arguments. {debug_port} will be replaced with the
    value of the --debug-port option.
```

I think either these options need to be promoted to the task level, or there needs to be some way to figure out what options the JVM sub-system supports, i.e.:

```
[prometheus source2 (master)]$ ./pants jvm -h

Unknown goal: jvm
```

This is discussed further here:

https://groups.google.com/forum/#!topic/pants-devel/fPyKDLnGOKI

issue_title: Support Ragel
issue_body: This was requested by a user.

issue_title: Memoize/precompute relevant TaskNode for each SelectNode
issue_body:
[Experiments show](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/compare/master...twitter:stuhood/engine-memoize-selectnode) that memoizing this catches about half of the cases where we would otherwise create subgraphs of Noops, and improves the total runtime by about 12%.

The reason memoization only catches half the cases is that we execute the graph breadth-first, so multiple attempts to compute the value have already begun by the time we record the memoized solution.

Computing this up front would mean splitting out (or reimplementing) the walking of `Selectors` to recursively determine viable TaskNodes to compute a particular SelectNode.

cc @ttim @baroquebobcat

issue_title: The go fetchers should support launchpad.net
issue_body:
The launchpad.net repos traditionally hosted some important go packages.  Even though most (even all) of these have migrated to github, folks are still out there with code that depends on the old launchpad versions, so pants should support their url scheme natively.

I added a custom setup for Medium like so:
```ini
[fetchers]
# NB: We are only adding the launchpad.net mapping below to the existing defaults.
# The existing defaults were copied from the default value displayed by:
#   `./pants help-advanced fetchers`
# TODO(John Sirois): Kill the custom mappings once XXX
# is available in a pants upgrade.
mapping: {
    r'bitbucket\.org/.*': 'ArchiveFetcher',
    r'golang\.org/x/.*': 'ArchiveFetcher',
    r'github\.com/.*': 'ArchiveFetcher',
    r'gopkg\.in/.*':'GopkgInFetcher'

    # Our custom mapping.
    r'launchpad\.net/.*': 'ArchiveFetcher',
  }


[archive-fetcher]
# NB: We are only adding the launchpad.net matcher below to the existing defaults.
# The existing defaults were copied from the default value displayed by:
#   `./pants help-advanced archive-fetcher`
# TODO(John Sirois): Kill the custom matchers once XXX
# is available in a pants upgrade.
matchers: {
    r'github\.com/(?P<user>[^/]+)/(?P<repo>[^/]+)':
      ('https://github.com/\\g<user>/\\g<repo>/archive/{rev}.tar.gz', 'master', 1),
    r'bitbucket\.org/(?P<user>[^/]+)/(?P<repo>[^/]+)':
      ('https://bitbucket.org/\\g<user>/\\g<repo>/get/{rev}.tar.gz', 'tip', 1),
    r'golang\.org/x/(?P<repo>[^/]+)':
      ('https://github.com/golang/\\g<repo>/archive/{rev}.tar.gz', 'master', 1),
   
    # Our custom matcher. 
    # NB: the '?ext=.tar.gz' URL suffix is made-up syntax ignored by launchpad.  It satisfies the
    # archive fetcher only - which decides archive type, and thus the means to unpack, via the
    # extension found in the url.
    r'launchpad\.net/(?P<repo>[^/]+)':
      ('https://bazaar.launchpad.net/~\\g<repo>/\\g<repo>/trunk/tarball/{rev}?ext=.tar.gz', '1', 3)
  }
```

issue_title: Unintentional cache invalidation in a dependency
issue_body:
I just opened https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/pull/2606 which has an example of how to reproduce a problem we are seeing at Square with cache invalidation in an internal library.

This is one example, I see many different zinc cached versions of the same internal library due to a problem like this, I'm assuming this is not the only situation like this.

Essentially, there is jar published (jetty-jsp) with a dependency on a library (jetty-schema)
There is another jar published with (jetty-server) that has a dependency on jetty-jsp.

```
$ ./pants clean-all
$ ./pants compile testprojects/src/java/org/pantsbuild/testproject/inccompile/libwithjettydep -ldebug >/tmp/c1 2>&1
$ ./pants compile testprojects/src/java/org/pantsbuild/testproject/inccompile/libwithjettyserver -ldebug >/tmp/c2 2>&1
```

This ends up with 2 different versions of the libwithjettydep directory being created:

```
ls -l .pants.d/compile/jvm/zinc/testprojects.src.java.org.pantsbuild.testproject.inccompile.libwithjettydep.libwithjettydep/
total 0
drwxr-xr-x  6 zundel  zundel  204 Nov 22 14:25 530bbd2ed61f
drwxr-xr-x  6 zundel  zundel  204 Nov 22 14:26 fc13d6991c18
```

The reason is because the classpath passed into the compiler is in a different order.  Here's some sanitized output from the debug log from launching the compiler.  


from /tmp/c1
```
--classpath
...
.pants.d/ivy/jars/org.eclipse.jetty/jetty-jsp/jars/jetty-jsp-9.2.9.v20150224.jar:
.pants.d/ivy/jars/org.eclipse.jetty.orbit/org.eclipse.jdt.core/orbits/org.eclipse.jdt.core-3.8.2.v20130121.jar:
.pants.d/ivy/jars/org.glassfish.web/javax.servlet.jsp.jstl/jars/javax.servlet.jsp.jstl-1.2.2.jar:
.pants.d/ivy/jars/org.eclipse.jetty.orbit/javax.servlet.jsp.jstl/orbits/javax.servlet.jsp.jstl-1.2.0.v201105211821.jar:
.pants.d/ivy/jars/org.glassfish.web/javax.servlet.jsp/jars/javax.servlet.jsp-2.3.2.jar:
.pants.d/ivy/jars/org.glassfish/javax.el/jars/javax.el-3.0.0.jar:
.pants.d/ivy/jars/javax.servlet.jsp/javax.servlet.jsp-api/jars/javax.servlet.jsp-api-2.3.1.jar:
.pants.d/ivy/jars/javax.servlet/javax.servlet-api/jars/javax.servlet-api-3.1.0.jar:
.pants.d/ivy/jars/org.eclipse.jetty.toolchain/jetty-schemas/jars/jetty-schemas-3.1.M0.jar 
```

From /tmp/c2:

```
-classpath ... 
.pants.d/ivy/jars/org.eclipse.jetty/jetty-jsp/jars/jetty-jsp-9.2.9.v20150224.jar:
.pants.d/ivy/jars/org.eclipse.jetty.orbit/org.eclipse.jdt.core/orbits/org.eclipse.jdt.core-3.8.2.v20130121.jar:
.pants.d/ivy/jars/org.glassfish.web/javax.servlet.jsp.jstl/jars/javax.servlet.jsp.jstl-1.2.2.jar:
.pants.d/ivy/jars/org.eclipse.jetty.orbit/javax.servlet.jsp.jstl/orbits/javax.servlet.jsp.jstl-1.2.0.v201105211821.jar:
.pants.d/ivy/jars/org.glassfish.web/javax.servlet.jsp/jars/javax.servlet.jsp-2.3.2.jar:

issue_title: Accept source_root definitions in pants.ini
issue_body:
Today these are defined in BUILD files which must then be registered as `--goals-bootstrap-buildfiles` in pants.ini.  This is a 2 step process that is confusing to the end user, and its an abuse of BUILD files to store non-target-graph configuration data.

For large organizations with - for example - multi-module maven project layouts, this list will get very large (>100s of entries).  In these cases something like #2143 will be needed such that this could be the configuration:
```ini
source_roots: @%(buildroot)s/source_roots.cfg
```

issue_title: SimpleTable does not support parameterized ProvisionedThroughput r/w capacity
issue_body:
If you create a template that uses parameters to allow the user to specify read/write capacity, the template will fail validation:

    SimpleTableExample:
        Type: 'AWS::Serverless::SimpleTable'
        Properties:
          PrimaryKey:
              Name: MyPrimaryKey
              Type: String
          ProvisionedThroughput:
            ReadCapacityUnits: !Ref ReadCapacityUnitsParam
            WriteCapacityUnits: !Ref WriteCapacityUnitsParam

Generates the error
`Resource with id [SimpleTableExample] is invalid. Type of property 'ProvisionedThroughput' is invalid.`

I suspect that this is due to the fact that !Ref-ed parameters are converted to string and the schema for the SimpleTable resource specifies the r/w capacity as a number. Hard-coding numbers in there works (but is not desired).

Is there a workaround for this?

issue_title: Specifying RestApiId for EventSource will not update Lambda policy to let RestApiId Gateway invoke Lambda
issue_body:
Flow to reproduce
## Using SAM create Lambda and add API Gateway Event to trigger it 

```
GetHelloWorld:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Properties:
      Handler: index.get
      Runtime: nodejs4.3
      Role: LambdaExecutionRole
      Events:
        GetEvent:
          Type: Api
          Properties:
            Path: /test
            Method: get
```
Expected result: 
1. Lambda is created
2. API Gateway (InitialAPIGateway) is created
3. "/test" endpoint is created
4. InitialAPIGateway is granted permission to invoke lambda

## Next step - we want to add swagger based API Gateway (to add CORS).

API Gateway Resource is added to SAM
```
  ApiGatewayApi:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Api
    Properties:
      StageName: Prod
      DefinitionBody:
        'Fn::Transform':
          Name: 'AWS::Include'
          Parameters:
            Location: s3://<bucket_name>/swagger.yaml

```
Swagger file  similar to one in examples folder is uploaded to bucket

Lambda is updated to reference created API Gateway
```
GetHelloWorld:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Properties:
      Handler: index.get
      Runtime: nodejs4.3
      Role: LambdaExecutionRole
      Events:
        GetEvent:
          Type: Api
          ---->>>RestApiId: !Ref ApiGatewayApi
          Properties:
            Path: /test
            Method: get
```

Expected result:
1. Lambda is updated
2. API Gateway (SwaggerBasedGateway) is created
3. "/test" endpoint is created in SwaggerBasedGateway
4. SwaggerBasedGateway is granted permission to invoke lambda

Unexpected behavior:
1. Two API Gateways is created - InitialGateway and SwaggerBasedGateway
2. Lambda policy has rules for invocation by InitialGateway but has no rules for invocation by SwaggerBasedGateway. Lambda policy is not updated to allow invocation from Swagger generated api.

In [other issue](https://github.com/awslabs/serverless-application-model/issues/59#issuecomment-348709355) it was mentioned 
> If you are using separate Swagger, make sure you add the APIs to the events section as well. This will make SAM add correct Lambda::Permissions. See https://github.com/awslabs/serverless-application-model/blob/master/examples/2016-10-31/api_swagger_cors/template.yaml#L24-L35

This doesn't seem to work, as after referencing API in event, Lambda permission is not updated to be invoked from new API.
 
```
Events:
        GetEvent:
          Type: Api
          RestApiId: !Ref ApiGatewayApi
```

Admitting this is not known issue when Lambda can not be invoked from Gateway API Console.
In this case Curl gives the same result as function policy is not updated.
Additionally I am not sure if this use-case should result in deploying 2 Gateway APIs once I reference RestApiId created from Swagger in event source. 

Adding permissions to invoke Lambda in SAM is not an approach as long as it must be defined for each Lambda in template separately as FunctionName property doesnt accept array. 

```
  ConfigLambdaPermission:
    Type: "AWS::Lambda::Permission"
    DependsOn:
    - GetHelloWorld
    Properties:
      Action: lambda:InvokeFunction
      FunctionName: !Ref GetHelloWorld
      Principal: apigateway.amazonaws.com
```

Using Kolibri devices as interim data syncs could disrupt user experience.
